title: Unrelated WIP work committed onto dummyindex's leftover chore/dummyindex-context-index branch in a submodule
evidence: ASSISTANT: "I'll commit the documentation changes. First let me see the exact working-tree state (we're inside the `backend` submodule, on `chore/dummyindex-context-index` — not the default branch, so no new branch needed)." ... "[chore/dummyindex-context-index 1b158de] docs: WIP brand-centric domain model rewrite + DB design-review findings | 9 files changed, 790 insertions(+), 295 deletions(-)"
what_happened: The backend (a git submodule of BOS-Mono) was still checked out on the branch dummyindex created for its index commit. The entire session — domain-model doc rewrite, council findings, the brand-centric-migration proposal — happened and was committed there; the assistant even treated 'not the default branch' as license to keep committing. The commit also swept up three pre-existing staged changes the assistant 'didn't author'. The index branch is now entangled with unrelated product-doc work inside a submodule, complicating merge of either.
root_cause_guess: dummyindex's ingest/index flow creates a chore/dummyindex-context-index branch and leaves it checked out with no follow-up (merge, switch back, or warn), which is extra hazardous in submodules where branch state is easy to overlook.

title: preflight wrongly warns 'not a git repo — writes won't be reversible via git' inside a git submodule
evidence: "## ⚠ Warnings - not a git repo — writes won't be reversible via git. ... git: not a git repo" — assistant: "(The \"not a git repo\" warning is a false-ish positive: `backend/` is a submodule, so its `.git` is a file pointer, not a dir — your changes are still git-tracked.)"
what_happened: `dummyindex context preflight` on BOS-Mono/backend (a git submodule where .git is a gitfile pointer, not a directory) reported the tree as not a git repo and warned writes were irreversible. Git was in fact fully functional — the later disaster recovery depended on `git checkout HEAD -- .context/features/INDEX.json`. A user trusting this warning could have made the wrong recovery decision.
root_cause_guess: Git detection checks for a `.git` directory instead of using `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` (or handling the gitfile pointer that submodules/worktrees use).

title: git index.lock contention in the submodule superproject during post-install cleanup
evidence:
fatal: Unable to create '/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.git/modules/frontend/index.lock': File exists.

Another git process seems to be running in this repository, e.g.
an editor opened by 'git commit'.
what_happened: Immediately after the destructive install (which runs git operations and installs hooks), both `git checkout HEAD -- .context/` and the settings.json revert failed on a stale index.lock under the superproject's .git/modules/frontend (the repo is a git submodule of BOS-Mono). No git process was running; Claude had to rm -f the lock manually to proceed with recovery.
root_cause_guess: A dummyindex-spawned git process (install/hook auto-refresh) crashed or overlapped in the submodule's gitdir, leaving the lock; dummyindex doesn't serialize its git access or clean up locks in submodule layouts.

title: Monorepo/submodule root ambiguity: tooling wired in frontend/ but session rooted at BOS-Mono — user told to start a different session
evidence: "Yes, almost — exactly that, with three things to know: 1. **Run it from inside `frontend/`** (not the mono root): start a session in `frontend/` and run `/dummyindex-build illustrations-paper-rebuild`. The design tooling resolves all its paths against the frontend root."
what_happened: The repo is a mono root with the dummyindex-equipped frontend as a submodule. The plan/build skills, equipment, plugins, and .context all live under frontend/, but the session ran at the mono root. Claude had to warn the user to start a new session inside frontend/; the user instead ran /dummyindex-build in the same mono-root session, which 'worked' only because Bash had been cd'd into frontend — while harness-level wiring (plugins, project agents, the design-handoff skill) still resolved from the mono root, directly causing issues 2, 5 and 9.
root_cause_guess: dummyindex has no concept of an equipped subdirectory inside a larger session root — CLI calls follow cwd but harness registration (skills/agents/plugins in settings.json) follows the session root, and nothing detects or reconciles the mismatch.

title: preflight falsely reports 'not a git repo' for a directory that is on a git branch and later anchors to HEAD
evidence:
L38 preflight: "## ⚠ Warnings
- not a git repo — writes won't be reversible via git.
...
- git: not a git repo" — yet event metadata shows gitBranch "development" at the same cwd, and L113 reconcile-stamp succeeds: "anchor advanced to a02e4370e939".
what_happened: `dummyindex context preflight` on BOS-Mono/backend (a directory inside the BOS-Mono monorepo, likely a subdir or submodule rather than a repo root) warned that writes wouldn't be git-reversible, while the same tree was demonstrably on branch `development` and git anchoring worked minutes later. The false warning misinforms the safety disclosure preflight exists to provide. (Preflight ran under the old 0.15.0 binary; may persist in newer versions.)
root_cause_guess: Git detection checks for a `.git` directory at the target root instead of `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`, failing for monorepo subdirectories / submodules (where `.git` is absent or a file).

title: SessionStart drift hook and Stop reconcile-gate are invisible to parent-repo (BOS-Mono root) sessions — submodule-only wiring
evidence: backend/.claude/settings.json wires '# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH ... dummyindex context plan-update --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"' (SessionStart) and 'dummyindex context reconcile-gate --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"' (Stop). The parent /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.claude/settings.json contains no dummyindex hooks (its SessionStart runs scripts/git-mode.sh), while BOS-Mono/CLAUDE.md says 'dummyindex is each submodule's workflow + plugin manager. Feature work in a submodule goes through ... /dummyindex-plan → /dummyindex-build'.
what_happened: Claude Code only loads .claude/settings.json from the session's project root. The parent monorepo clearly hosts sessions at its root (it has its own .claude/ with agents, hooks, skills and a CLAUDE.md that mandates the dummyindex workflow for submodules), but for such sessions the backend submodule's drift report, memory hooks, and the always-on Stop reconcile-gate never fire — backend edits from a mono-root session bypass the gate silently. The hooks only work when a session is opened with backend/ itself as cwd. dummyindex has no parent-level wiring story for submodules.
root_cause_guess: Hook installer assumes session root == indexed repo root; for a submodule it would need a parent-scope hook (or documented limitation) that invokes `dummyindex context plan-update/reconcile-gate --root <submodule>` per indexed submodule.

title: Submodule wiring: hooks correct inside frontend/, but the BOS-Mono parent root has zero dummyindex wiring, so monorepo-rooted sessions bypass the drift report and reconcile gate
evidence: frontend/.claude/settings.json wires SessionStart (plan-update, memory session-start), Stop (memory nudge, reconcile-gate), PreCompact, PostToolUse prettier — all with `--root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"`. Parent /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.claude/settings.json has its own SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks (git-mode.sh, docs-frontmatter-guard.mjs) and no DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH entries; BOS-Mono/.context is a different, non-dummyindex folder ('Docs + plain-markdown agent memory', no HOW_TO_USE.md).
what_happened: Inside the submodule everything works — plan-update ran live, the gitdir-pointer .git file is handled, the anchor commit (1b7fa81f) resolves and is an ancestor of HEAD. But the documented workflow ('mounted at ../backend/ when inside the BOS-Mono monorepo') means sessions are routinely rooted at BOS-Mono, where frontend/.claude/settings.json is never loaded: no drift report, no Stop reconcile-gate, no managed CLAUDE.md block for the indexed submodule. The index drifts silently for exactly the sessions most likely to edit across frontend+backend. Also, the parent's unrelated `.context/` collides with the global-CLAUDE.md rule 'read .context/HOW_TO_USE.md' (the file doesn't exist there).
root_cause_guess: dummyindex install wires hooks only at the indexed root and has no submodule/monorepo story (e.g. offering to add a parent-scoped hook with --root pointing at the submodule, or detecting that the indexed root is a submodule and warning).

title: Monorepo sibling (frontend) invisible to dummyindex: breaking cross-repo impact handled off-checklist with a generic agent
evidence: Probe: '=== has its own .context? ===' (empty) ... dispatch: 'subagent_type: Frontend Developer'
what_happened: Backend proposal broke the sibling frontend's API contracts; frontend had no .context/equipment, so the migration was improvised off-checklist with a generic agent. The user's later runtime error arose at exactly this seam.
root_cause_guess: Index and equipment are single-repo scoped; plans cannot declare or track cross-repo consumers of a breaking change.
